data.name: Discharge from CRU after Inpatient Period 1
Return to CRU for Inpatient Period 2
data.valueLabel: Return to CRU for Inpatient Period 2

data.name: I/O Collection Period 3 - 15 mg SS and 30 mg
Predose PK/PD Sampling - 15 mg Steady State
data.valueLabel: Predose PK/PD Sampling - 15 mg Steady State

data.name: SRC Safety Data Review Through Day 23
Overnight Fast Before Dosing in CRU
Urine Void Within 2 Hours of 24-Hour Period Completion
Fasting Before Serum Chemistry at Screening/Check-in
SRC Safety Review Before Dose Titration
data.description: Safety Review Committee reviews available safety data through Day 23 for each participant before titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Before titration to 30 mg/day, the Safety Review Committee (SRC) will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: ALT Elevation > 5x ULN
data.description: ALT elevation greater than 5 times the upper limit of normal triggers dose modification
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when ALT is ≤ 3 × ULN.

data.name: ALT Elevation > 3x ULN
data.description: ALT elevation greater than 3 times ULN triggers dose reduction or restriction
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality.

data.name: Hemoglobin or Platelet Decrease > 30% from Baseline
data.description: Greater than 30% decrease from baseline in hemoglobin or platelets triggers dose modification
data.text: Hemoglobin or Platelets > 30% decrease from baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality.

data.name: Neutrophil Decrease > 30% from Baseline with Below-Range Baseline
data.description: Greater than 30% decrease from baseline in neutrophils when neutrophils are below reference range at baseline
data.text: Neutrophils > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range at baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.description: Bilirubin greater than 2 times ULN accompanied by ALT greater than 3 times ULN indicative of liver injury
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN, indicative of liver injury: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when bilirubin is below ULN. Rechallenge requires approval of the Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Neurological Worsening
data.description: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or by neurologic physical exam assessment
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening by AEs or by neurologic physical exam assessment: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Psychiatric Worsening
data.description: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening including suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening which may include but not limited to suicidality, acute depression, or psychosis: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification.

data.name: Serious Hypersensitivity Reaction
data.description: Occurrence of serious hypersensitivity reaction requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction

data.name: Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.description: Occurrence of severe uncontrolled infection requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection

data.name: Use of Disallowed Medication
data.description: Use of disallowed medication requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Use of disallowed medication

data.name: Pregnancy or Planned Pregnancy
data.description: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires discontinuation consideration
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy

data.name: Clinically Significant QTcF Change
data.description: Clinically significant change from baseline in QTcF after enrollment
data.text: If a clinically significant finding is identified (including, but not limited to changes from baseline in QT interval corrected using Fridericia's formula [QTcF]) after enrollment, the Investigator or qualified designee will determine if the participant can continue in the study.

data.name: Safety Concern Preventing Dose Titration
data.description: Safety concerns that may place participants at undue risk prevent titration to 30 mg/day
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study.

data.name: Maximum Rechallenge Limit
data.description: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed for dose modifications
data.text: A maximum of 3 rechallenges will be allowed.

data.name: Enrollment to Run-in Period
data.text: Participants eligible for the study will be enrolled on Day -7 and initiated on a copper- and molybdenum-controlled diet. Participants will be admitted to the CRU on Day -8.

data.name: Run-in to Treatment Period 1 (15 mg/day)
data.text: Following the Run-in Period, participants will be administered ALXN1840 at 15 mg/day starting Day 1 for approximately 28 days.

data.name: Treatment Period 1 to Outpatient Period
data.text: Participants may be discharged from the CRU on Day 9 to return on Day 22 or Day 23 (predose). The Outpatient Period may be extended up to an additional 14 days with Investigator approval.

data.name: Outpatient Period to Inpatient Period 2
data.text: Participants who are discharged will be re-admitted on Day 22 or Day 23 for Treatment Period 2 and will remain in house until Day 40. All procedures will start on Day 23.

data.name: Dose Titration from 15 mg to 30 mg
data.text: Participants will be administered ALXN1840 at a dose of 15 mg/day on Day 1 through Day 28 and then increase to 30 mg/day on Day 29 through Day 39. Before titration to 30 mg/day, the SRC will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant.

data.name: Dose Reduction for ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: ALT > 3 × ULN: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution of abnormality. Weekly repeat testing.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for ALT > 5x ULN
data.text: ALT > 5 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when ALT is ≤ 3 × ULN.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Hemoglobin/Platelet Decrease > 30%
data.text: Hemoglobin or Platelets > 30% decrease from baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD. No further dose increase until resolution.

data.name: Temporary Interruption for Bilirubin > 2x ULN with ALT > 3x ULN
data.text: Bilirubin > 2 × ULN accompanied by ALT > 3 × ULN: Temporary interruption. Weekly repeat testing. Rechallenge at 15 mg QOD or less frequent, when bilirubin is below ULN. Requires approval of Alexion Medical Monitor.

data.name: Dose Modification for Neurological Worsening
data.text: Evidence of neurologic worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Dose Modification for Psychiatric Worsening
data.text: Evidence of clinically significant acute psychiatric worsening: Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor will evaluate the need for dose modification (interruption, increase or decrease) based on copper control parameters and relevant clinical data.

data.name: Discontinuation for Serious Hypersensitivity
data.text: Serious hypersensitivity reaction requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Severe Uncontrolled Infection
data.text: Severe uncontrolled infection requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Disallowed Medication Use
data.text: Use of disallowed medication requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Pregnancy
data.text: Pregnancy or planned pregnancy requires consideration for discontinuation from study intervention.

data.name: Discontinuation for Abnormal Liver Function
data.text: Discontinuation of study intervention for abnormal liver function should be considered by the Investigator when a participant meets one of the conditions outlined in the Dose Modification criteria (Section 6.6) or if the Investigator believes that it is in best interest of the participant.

data.name: Treatment Completion to Post-Study Therapy
data.text: Following completion of both inpatient periods of the study (ie, Day 40), participants will either transition to therapy that was discontinued before enrollment, transition to other standard of care therapy, or consider continuation of ALXN1840 treatment.

data.name: Remain on 15 mg Due to Safety Concerns
data.text: Unless there are safety concerns that, in the opinion of the Investigator(s) and/or Alexion, may place participants at undue risk, in which case, participants may remain on the 15 mg/day dose for the duration of the study.

data.name: Dose Reduction for Neutrophil Decrease
data.text: Neutrophils > 30% decrease from baseline with neutrophils below reference range at baseline: Reduce dose to previous dose level if up-titration has occurred or reduce dose to 15 mg QOD if on 15 mg QD.

data.name: Participant Voluntary Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may withdraw from the study at any time at his/her own request.

data.name: Investigator-Initiated Withdrawal
data.description: A participant may be withdrawn at any time at the discretion of the Investigator for safety, behavioral, compliance, or administrative reasons.

data.name: Study Completion
data.description: A participant is considered to have completed the study if he/she has completed all phases of the study including the last scheduled procedure shown in the Schedule of Activities. End of Study Visit on Day 54 (+/- 2 days).

data.name: Lost to Follow-up
data.description: A participant will be considered lost to follow-up if he or she repeatedly fails to return for scheduled visits and is unable to be contacted by the study site. At least 3 telephone calls and a certified letter must be attempted before deeming lost to follow-up.

data.name: Study Intervention Discontinuation - Remains in Study
data.description: If the study intervention is definitively discontinued, the participant should remain in the study to be evaluated for safety follow-up.

data.name: Alexion Study Termination
data.description: Alexion reserves the right to close the study site or terminate the study at any time for any reason at the sole discretion of Alexion.

data.name: Investigator-Initiated Site Closure
data.description: The Investigator may initiate study-site closure at any time, provided there is reasonable cause and sufficient notice is given in advance of the intended termination.

data.name: SRC Safety Review Before Dose Titration
data.description: Before titration to 30 mg/day on Day 29, the Safety Review Committee will review available safety data through Day 23 for each participant to determine if dose escalation is appropriate.

data.name: ALT-Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: At any safety assessment, ALT levels are evaluated. ALT > 3x ULN triggers dose reduction; ALT > 5x ULN triggers temporary interruption with rechallenge when ALT ≤ 3x ULN.

data.name: Hematology-Based Dose Modification Decision
data.description: At any safety assessment, hemoglobin, platelets, and neutrophils are evaluated. Greater than 30% decrease from baseline triggers dose reduction.

data.name: Liver Injury Assessment Decision
data.description: Bilirubin > 2x ULN accompanied by ALT > 3x ULN triggers temporary interruption. Rechallenge requires Alexion Medical Monitor approval.

data.name: Neurological/Psychiatric Assessment Decision
data.description: Evidence of neurologic or psychiatric worsening requires evaluation by Investigator and Alexion Medical Monitor for dose modification based on copper control parameters and clinical data.

data.name: QTcF Assessment Decision
data.description: If clinically significant QTcF change is identified after enrollment, the Investigator determines if the participant can continue and if any change in management is needed.

data.name: Outpatient Period Extension Decision
data.description: At Day 9, the Investigator may approve extension of the Outpatient Period up to an additional 14 days. Participants will be given additional investigational product for daily dosing.

data.name: CRU Retention During Outpatient Period Decision
data.description: At the CRU's discretion, participants may remain in the CRU during the outpatient period for safety or to maintain the integrity of the conduct of the study.

data.name: Discontinuation Assessment Decision
data.description: At any time during the study, participants must be considered for discontinuation if serious hypersensitivity, severe uncontrolled infection, disallowed medication use, or pregnancy occurs.
